title: UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 45 Translator, Assistant Mining Engineer and More Posts
short_info: Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has published Advertisement No. 04/2026 inviting online recruitment applications (ORA) for direct recruitment by selection to various prestigious Group ‘A’ and Group ‘B’ Gazetted posts under different Ministries and Departments of the Government of India. Applications are invited from eligible Indian citizens for filling up these posts on a permanent basis. Interested and eligible candidates can submit their applications online through the UPSC website https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/. The last date for submission of online applications is 29th May 2026 (18

application_fee.details: For all Candidates: Rs. 25/- For SC/ST/PwBD/Women candidates: NIL | Details of application fee will be available in the online application portal. Candidates are advised to check the instructions while filling the form. Fee exemption is applicable as per UPSC rules for SC/ST/PwBD/Female candidates (wherever notified).

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill the essential educational qualifications and experience as mentioned against each post. Detailed qualifications are available in the official notification. Qualifications are relaxable at the discretion of UPSC for otherwise well-qualified candidates. | Key Highlights: | Engineering Faculty Posts require B.E./B.Tech + M.E./M.Tech with first class in relevant discipline. Translator Posts require Bachelor’s Degree in the concerned foreign language or equivalent diploma with English. Administrative & Other Posts require Bachelor’s Degree with relevant experience. PwBD candidates must meet the specified disability criteria.

vacancy_details.Post Name & Organization: Assistant Library & Information Officer (Language – Kannada), Central Secretariat Library, Ministry of Culture
Associate Professor, Civil Engineering (Structural), College of Military Engineering, Pune, Ministry of Defence
Assistant Professor, Civil Engineering (Structural), College of Military Engineering, Pune
Assistant Professor, Electrical Engineering (Power System), College of Military Engineering, Pune
Assistant Professor, Mechanical Engineering (Heat Transfer), College of Military Engineering, Pune
Translator (Chinese), Signal Intelligence Directorate, Integrated Defence Staff HQ, Ministry of Defence
Translator (Tibetan), Signal Intelligence Directorate, Integrated Defence Staff HQ, Ministry of Defence
Senior Administrative Officer Grade-II, DRDO, Ministry of Defence
Assistant Mining Engineer, Indian Bureau of Mines, Ministry of Mines
Assistant Research Officer, Indian Bureau of Mines, Ministry of Mines
Accounts Officer, Finance Department, Administration of UT of Ladakh

category_wise_vacancy.Post Name & Organization: Accounts Officer, Finance Department, Administration of UT of Ladakh

selection_process: Selection will be made on the basis of: - Shortlisting based on essential/desirable qualifications and experience. - Recruitment Test (if conducted). - Personal Interview. Final merit list will be prepared after the interview. Minimum qualifying marks apply for each category.
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply online only using the UPSC ORA portal: https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/. - First register/create profile (URN). - Fill Common Application Form (CAF). - Fill post-specific module. - Upload required documents, photograph, and signature. - Pay fee (if applicable) and submit.
Educational certificates, experience certificates, caste/PwBD certificates, etc. in PDF format.
Before logging into the portal, ensure you have the following ready:
Valid Email ID and Mobile Number – These will be used for all future communication and OTP verification.

Scanned Documents (in PDF format, preferably under 1 MB each): Recent passport-size photograph Signature Matriculation/10th certificate (for Date of Birth proof) All educational qualification certificates and mark sheets Experience certificates (if applicable) Caste/Category certificate (OBC/EWS/SC/ST) PwBD certificate (if applicable) Any other supporting documents mentioned in the notification
Matriculation/10th certificate (for Date of Birth proof)
All educational qualification certificates and mark sheets
Any other supporting documents mentioned in the notification
Details of all educational qualifications and experience chronologically.
Credit/Debit Card or Net Banking details for payment of application fee (if applicable).
important_instructions: Applications received after the closing date or incomplete in any respect will be summarily rejected. Candidates must upload clear scanned copies of all supporting documents. Date of birth mentioned in Matriculation certificate will be considered final. Shortlisted candidates will be called for interview and must bring original documents. Canvassing in any form will disqualify the candidature. UPSC reserves the right to cancel/withdraw any post without assigning any reason.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for UPSC Advertisement No. 04/2026 Recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form for UPSC Advertisement No. 04/2026 Recruitment is 29 May 2026 up to 06:00 PM. Candidates interested in applying for various Group ‘A’ and Group ‘B’ Gazetted posts must complete the online registration process before the deadline through the official UPSC ORA portal.

faq.question: Q2. Which posts are available under UPSC Advertisement No. 04/2026?
faq.answer: UPSC has released vacancies for several prestigious posts including Assistant Library & Information Officer, Associate Professor, Assistant Professors in Engineering disciplines, Translator (Chinese & Tibetan), Senior Administrative Officer, Assistant Mining Engineer, Assistant Research Officer, and Accounts Officer. These vacancies are available under different Ministries and Departments of the Government of India.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply only through online mode using the official UPSC Online Recruitment Application (ORA) portal. No offline application forms or other modes of submission will be accepted by the Union Public Service Commission. Applicants are advised to carefully fill the Common Application Form and upload all required documents before final submission

faq.question: Q4. What educational qualifications are required for UPSC Advertisement No. 04/2026 posts?
faq.answer: Educational qualifications vary depending on the post applied for. Engineering faculty posts generally require B.E./B.Tech and M.E./M.Tech degrees in relevant disciplines, while Translator posts require proficiency in foreign languages like Chinese or Tibetan. Administrative and research-related posts require graduation along with relevant experience as specified in the official notification.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for UPSC Advertisement No. 04/2026 Recruitment?
faq.answer: The upper age limit differs according to the post and category. Most vacancies have age limits ranging between 30 and 45 years. Reserved category candidates including OBC, SC, ST, and PwBD applicants are eligible for age relaxation benefits as per Government of India and UPSC recruitment rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for UPSC Advertisement No. 04/2026?
faq.answer: The selection process may include shortlisting of candidates based on educational qualifications and experience, Recruitment Test (if conducted), and Personal Interview. Final merit will be prepared based on candidate performance in the interview and other recruitment stages conducted by UPSC

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee exemption in UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, fee exemption benefits are available for eligible categories such as SC, ST, PwBD, and Female candidates according to UPSC recruitment rules. The exact fee details and payment instructions will be available during the online application process on the UPSC ORA portal.

faq.question: Q8. How can candidates apply for UPSC Advertisement No. 04/2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must first create a profile or URN on the UPSC ORA portal and complete the Common Application Form. After selecting the desired post, applicants need to upload scanned documents, photograph, signature, and pay the application fee if applicable before submitting the form online.

faq.question: Q9. What documents are required during UPSC online application submission?
faq.answer: Applicants need to upload educational certificates, experience certificates, caste certificates, PwBD certificates, and other supporting documents in PDF format. Candidates should ensure that all uploaded documents are clear and valid because incomplete or incorrect documentation may lead to rejection of the application form.

faq.question: Q10. What important instructions should candidates remember before applying for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates should carefully verify eligibility criteria, upload accurate documents, and complete the application process before the deadline. Any incomplete application or submission after the closing date will be rejected. UPSC may also cancel or withdraw any vacancy without assigning reasons, and candidates involved in canvassing may face disqualification.
